constellations.title: CRDTs Are Eating Everything
constellations.explanation: Three unrelated projects — a mobile database, a design tool, and a notebook — independently chose CRDTs as their sync primitive. This isn't coincidence: it signals that real-time collaboration is becoming a baseline expectation, and CRDTs are winning the architectural debate over OT.

constellations.title: The Privacy-By-Architecture Movement
constellations.explanation: Differential privacy analytics, decentralized identity, and decentralized storage each solve privacy from a different angle — statistical, identity, and data sovereignty. Together they sketch a world where privacy isn't a feature toggle but a structural guarantee baked into the system architecture.

constellations.title: From Review to Agent to Guardian
constellations.explanation: Code review bots lead to autonomous coding agents, which create the need for supply chain attack detection. This is an evolutionary chain: as AI writes more code, the attack surface grows, demanding AI-powered security to close the loop.

constellations.title: The Compute Location Spectrum
constellations.explanation: Edge WASM runtime, microcontroller ML, browser-local translation, and voice-on-device form a spectrum of where compute happens — from cloud to edge to browser to chip. The axis reveals a strong pull toward pushing intelligence to the smallest possible device.

constellations.title: Missing: The Personal API Layer
constellations.explanation: A knowledge graph from browsing, semantic doc search, and an SQL learning platform all deal with making information accessible. What's conspicuously absent is a personal API that unifies all your scattered data — browsing, notes, bookmarks, conversations — into one queryable interface.

patterns.pattern_description: Multiple projects assume that cloud dependency is a bug, not a feature. From CRDTs to on-device ML to local-only voice assistants, there's a quiet paradigm shift: the default is moving from 'cloud with offline fallback' to 'local with optional sync'.

patterns.pattern_title: Developer Tools Are Becoming AI Consumers
patterns.pattern_description: Code review, testing, accessibility auditing, and even CI/CD are all converging on LLM integration. The implicit assumption is that every developer tool will eventually have an AI copilot layer — the question isn't if but when.
